acceptance_conditions.condition: protocol and source bindings remain exact
each full and halving repeat records 1,728 and 816 executions
both full repeats reproduce all 12 R142 selections
both halving repeats reproduce all 12 R143 selections
pooled execution-loop runtime reduction is at least 30 percent
each adjacent full/halving pair has at least 20 percent runtime reduction
the two paired runtime-reduction fractions differ by at most 15 percentage points
pooled halving/full per-execution runtime ratio lies between 0.5 and 2.0
schedule follows the post-preregistration secret and transcript hashes verify
cross-machine, calibration, hardware, advantage, BQP, solved-frontier, and credit claims remain false

claim_boundary.what_is_not_supported_even_if_accepted: cross-machine or calibration transfer, real hardware or cloud billing savings, protocol soundness, quantum advantage, BQP separation, or solved B4/B8/B10

phase_protocol: commit a fresh secret after public preregistration
derive ABBA or BAAB from the secret
perform shared untimed setup and warmup
time four fresh strategy runs in the secret-selected order
write all measurement records before secret reveal
reveal the secret and verify counts, selections, pairing, and hashes
research_question: Does R144 retain at least thirty percent pooled runtime savings after a secret-selected ABBA/BAAB repeated-order challenge?

shared_untimed_setup: compile the same 96 unique-QASM shortlist circuits
one simulator warmup circuit per backend snapshot
timed_scope: update online LCB statistics and eliminate candidates
